assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions testing on dev Rapid channel clusters first and tailors advice specifically for prod Stable channel clusters

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: Response mentions Autopilot handles node management and includes mandatory resource requests as a consideration, though SSH limitation not explicitly mentioned

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: Response includes checking Kubernetes 1.32 breaking changes and verifying no workloads using deprecated APIs, with specific kubectl commands for backup

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: Response specifically mentions reviewing workload resource requests/limits as Autopilot may have updated resource allocation rules

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: Response includes PodDisruptionBudgets verification and proper readiness/liveness probes, though bare pods and graceful shutdown not explicitly mentioned

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: Response includes specific kubectl version command and other verification commands like kubectl get nodes

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: Response includes comprehensive workload validation section with application health endpoints, service discovery, and persistent volume checks

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends testing on dev clusters first and manually upgrading dev Rapid channel clusters to 1.32

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the GKE release schedule or the ~1 month advance notice timing, only references the user's mention of next month notification
Response includes comprehensive monitoring sections with performance metrics, application response times, and logging pipeline validation

assertions.expectation: Does NOT proactively recommend node-level maintenance exclusions for Autopilot unless the customer explicitly asks for node upgrade control
assertions.reason: Response correctly does not mention node-level maintenance exclusions, focusing appropriately on workload-level concerns for Autopilot
